task_completion.description: Open the route evaluation mandate and NASA Panoply application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the contents of 'route_evaluation_mandate.txt' and the NASA Panoply application window open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the surface pressure and air temperature datasets into Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply 'Sources' or 'Datasets' panel showing 'pres.mon.ltm.nc' and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' loaded. The distractor file 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' should not be the active dataset.

task_completion.description: Create a map visualization of surface pressure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of surface pressure. The plot title, variable name, or legend should clearly indicate 'pres' or surface pressure (values in the Andes region should be visibly lower, around 500-750 mb/hPa).

task_completion.description: Create a map visualization of air temperature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of air temperature. The plot title, variable name, or legend should clearly indicate 'air' or air temperature.

task_completion.description: Draft the High/Hot hazard flight advisory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing a drafted advisory document that discusses high/hot hazards, density altitude, and references the analyzed surface pressure and temperature data.

task_completion.description: Export the maps and advisory text file to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or save dialog showing 'andes_surface_pressure_jan.png', 'andes_air_temperature_jan.png', and 'high_hot_advisory.txt' successfully saved in the '~/Documents/' or '~/Documents/AviationLogistics' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply graphical interface to generate the atmospheric maps.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Panoply GUI to configure and render the plots, rather than using terminal scripts (e.g., Python/matplotlib) to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the Sea Level Pressure distractor dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Panoply plot for pressure must be generated from 'pres.mon.ltm.nc' (Surface Pressure), not 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' (Sea Level Pressure). The plot metadata/title should reflect 'pres' rather than 'slp'.

integrity.description: The agent actually typed or generated the advisory text based on the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor should show the advisory being actively written or saved, containing specific references to the data (e.g., pressure ranges around 500-750 hPa), rather than a generic placeholder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing surface pressure and air temperature for the Andes region (10°N to 30°S, 85°W to 55°W) for January. The expected minimum surface pressure in this region is ~618 mb (hPa), which falls in the correct 500 to 750 hPa range. The expected output files are 'andes_surface_pressure_jan.png', 'andes_air_temperature_jan.png', and 'high_hot_advisory.txt', which must be saved in '~/Documents/AviationLogistics' (or '~/Documents/'). The agent must use 'pres.mon.ltm.nc' and 'air.mon.ltm.nc', and specifically avoid the distractor file 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' (Sea Level Pressure).
